top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY NYC (Gran Fondo New York)'s demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
75ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
6,371ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
GFNY NYC (Gran Fondo New York)'s rolling hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 85 miles.
At mile 60 of 85, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
At GFNY NYC (Gran Fondo New York), surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY NYC (Gran Fondo New York). Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on rolling hills. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY NYC (Gran Fondo New York), that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
6,371ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,371ft of climbing on rolling hills does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 85 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY NYC (Gran Fondo New York)’s 85 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY NYC (Gran Fondo New York) requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling hills to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, TT Threshold, and VO2max to prepare you for 85 miles of rolling hills in George Washington Bridge (NJ side) / Fort Lee, NJ.
